response:
The project is divided into five phases with man-hours following a geometric progression with a common ratio of 2. Let the first phase require x man-hours. The total man-hours for all phases is x + 2x + 4x + 8x + 16x = 31x. Given that the total is 310, 31x = 310. Solving for x gives x = 10. Therefore the man-hours for each phase are 10, 20, 40, 80, and 160.

The initial total project duration was 20 weeks. Each phase will now take 1.5 times longer than planned. The total duration is multiplied by 1.5. The new total project duration is 20 multiplied by 1.5. The result is 30 weeks. The increase in time does not affect the order of the phases. The project manager must adjust the timeline accordingly. The project manager must ensure all teams are aware of the new schedule. The project manager must monitor progress closely. The project manager must allocate resources efficiently. The project manager must maintain communication with all stakeholders. The project manager must ensure that the quality of work is not compromised. The project manager must track the progress of each phase. The project manager must be prepared for any further delays. The project manager must ensure that all tasks are completed on time. The project manager must maintain a structured approach to project management. The project manager must ensure that all team members are aligned with the new timeline. The project manager must be ready to make adjustments as needed. The project manager must ensure that the project stays on track. The project manager must ensure that the project meets its objectives. The project manager must ensure that the project is completed within the new timeline. The fortune of the project manager depends on effective planning and execution.
